zh.lifeSummary: 當Grace Alice Alcock於1601年4月13日出生在英國艾可拉馬緒時，她的父親Richard Alcock38歲，母親Ellen Tredwell33歲。她於1624年與David Carwithen在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1665年8月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Grace Alice Alcock于1601年4月13日出生在英国艾可拉馬緒时，她的父亲Richard Alcock38岁，母亲Ellen Tredwell33岁。她于1624年与David Carwithen在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1665年8月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: Alcock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Alcok, a pet form of any of the many medieval personal names beginning with Al-, especially Alan and Alexander. The Middle English hypocoristic suffix -cok (see Cocke ) was very commonly added to personal names in Middle English: compare for example Hancock and Wilcock .

variants.name: Cocke
meanings: nickname from Middle English cok (Old English and Old French coc) ‘rooster’.
patronymic from the Old French and Middle English personal name Cok, of uncertain origin but perhaps derived from the word in 1 above.

variants.name: Alcott
meanings: English: ostensibly a topographic name from any of a large number of places called in Old English as ‘the old cottage’ (Old English (e)ald + cot(e)). In fact, however, it is generally an altered form of Alcock , in part at least for euphemistic reasons.

History: Louisa May Alcott (1832–88), author of Little Women (1869), was the daughter of Amos Bronson Alcott (1799–1888), who had changed the surname from Alcox. The family trace their descent from an Alcocke family who emigrated from England to MA with John Winthrop (see Winthrop ) in 1630.

nameMeanings.name: Grace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun (via Old French, from Latin gratia), this name occurs occasionally in the 15th century, and by the 1540s was among the most popular girls' names in some parishes. It has always been particularly popular in Scotland and northern England (borne, for example, by Grace Darling , the lighthouse keeper's daughter whose heroism in 1838 , saving sailors in a storm, caught the popular imagination). In more recent times it was famous as the name of the actress Grace Kelly ( 1928–82 ), who became Princess Grace of Monaco. In Ireland it is used as an Anglicized form of Gráinne .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Grace Alice Alcock was born on 13 April 1601, in Lamarsh, Essex, England, her father, Richard Alcock, was 38 and her mother, Ellen Tredwell, was 33. She married David Carwithen in 1624, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 30 August 1665, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 64.

ja.lifeSummary: Grace Alice Alcockが1601年4月13日にイギリスエセックスラマーッシュで生まれた時，父Richard Alcockは38歳，母Ellen Tredwellは33歳であった。1624年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサレムでDavid Carwithenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1665年8月30日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサレムで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Grace Alice Alcock(이)가 1601년 4월 13일 영국 에세스 램아르시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Alcock(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen Tredwell(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1624년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘에서 David Carwithen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1665년 8월 30일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Grace Alice Alcock nasceu em 13 de abril de 1601, em Lamarsh, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Alcock, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Ellen Tredwell, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com David Carwithen em 1624, em Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de agosto de 1665, em Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Grace Alice Alcock родилась 13 апреля 1601 г. в местности лэмарш, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Alcock было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Ellen Tredwell, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David Carwithen в 1624 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 30 августа 1665 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Grace Alice Alcock nació el 13 de abril de 1601, en Lamarsh, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Alcock, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Ellen Tredwell, tenía 33 años. Se casó con David Carwithen en 1624, en Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 30 de agosto de 1665, en Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 64 años.
